title: Module 06 — Meshing
problem: Starting from the provided Globe_Valve_SS06_Start.wbpz archive, generate a mesh for the globe valve assembly: first the program-controlled default mesh, then a refined global mesh (Adaptive Sizing off, 6.35 mm global element size), then a local Body Sizing control on the valve body (3.175 mm) to further refine the mesh in that region, saving the project when done.

_notes.source: Built from ANSYS Mechanical Getting Started, Release 2023 R1 — Module 06 Step-by-Step Guide: Mesh.
_notes.selector_status: UNVERIFIED — selectors use display names/labels taken from the source slide screenshots, following the same convention as tut1.json. Resolve real UIA automation_id/name via live testing before relying on them.
_notes.highlight_legend: uia = overlay draws a box around a UIA element. ocr_text = overlay finds on-screen text via OCR (used for the Workbench Toolbox / Project Schematic, which are not exposed to UIA — see tut1.json's workbench_uia_finding note). instruction = 3D-viewport action UIA cannot see; shows a reference screenshot instead of a tracked box. none = no highlight (launch).
_notes.verify_legend: Mesh generation is a real, checkable model-state change (element/node counts, mesh existence), so those steps use verify.type == "script" (engine pymechanical) the way tut1.json does for solve/result steps. Steps with no clean automatable check (menu opens, field entry, save) use verify.type == "manual", matching tut1's manual_verify_switch_2026-06 decision for anything not worth chasing OCR/UIA fragility on for a student-facing pilot.
_notes.report_checks_omitted: No report_checks block: this module doesn't ask the student to compute or report a numeric result (it's a meshing exercise, not a solved analysis), so per the template's guidance the tutorial simply has no report-upload step.
_notes.wb_steps_reused_from_tut5: wb_01_open / wb_02_open_archive / wb_03_edit_model mirror the same three steps in tut5_connections.json (only the archive filename differs: Globe_Valve_SS06_Start.wbpz here vs. SS05 there), since both modules start the same way — open Workbench, open the starter archive, edit the Model cell into Mechanical.

sections.section: Open the starter project in Workbench

steps.title: Open Ansys Workbench
steps.description: Launch Ansys Workbench: Windows Start Menu button → All apps → ANSYS nn.n → Workbench nn.n.
hints: Start it from the Windows Start menu or the lab launcher.

steps.title: Open the starter archive
steps.description: File → Open…, browse for archive file Globe_Valve_SS06_Start.wbpz, and click Open.

verify.prompt: Project opened, showing the Static Structural system with Engineering Data and Geometry already checked?

hints: Click File to open the menu, then choose Open…

steps.title: Open Mechanical
steps.description: Right-click the Model cell in the Static Structural system block and choose Edit… to launch Ansys Mechanical.
hints: The system block is in the Project Schematic; 'Model' is the row with the lightning-bolt icon.

steps.title: Generate the default mesh
steps.description: Right-click Mesh in the Outline tree and choose Generate Mesh to create the program-controlled default mesh.
hints: Right-click directly on the 'Mesh' branch in the Outline tree.

steps.title: Select the Mesh branch
steps.description: Select Mesh in the Outline tree to show its Details panel.

verify.prompt: Details of "Mesh" panel showing Defaults and Sizing sections?

hints: The Details panel appears at the bottom-left when Mesh is selected.

steps.title: Turn off Use Adaptive Sizing
steps.description: In Details of Mesh → Sizing, set Use Adaptive Sizing to No.

verify.prompt: Use Adaptive Sizing set to No?

hints: Use Adaptive Sizing is a dropdown in the Sizing section of Details.

steps.title: Set the global Element Size to 6.35 mm
steps.description: In Details of Mesh → Defaults, set Element Size to 6.35 mm.
hints: Element Size is in the Defaults section, above Sizing.

steps.title: Generate Mesh with the new global size
steps.description: Right-click Mesh in the Outline tree and choose Generate Mesh again to apply the 6.35 mm global element size.
hints: Same right-click menu as the first mesh generation.

sections.section: Add local Body Sizing on the valve body

steps.title: Insert a Body Sizing control
steps.description: Right-click Mesh in the Outline tree → Insert → Sizing to add a Body Sizing control.
hints: 'Sizing' is under the Insert submenu when right-clicking Mesh.

steps.title: Scope Body Sizing to the valve body
steps.description: Select the valve body in the Geometry view, then click Apply next to detail Geometry in the Body Sizing Details so Geometry reads '1 Body'.

verify.prompt: Detail Geometry now shows '1 Body' (the valve body)?

hints: Click the Body Sizing branch first so its Details panel and Geometry field are active.

steps.title: Set the Body Sizing Element Size to 3.175 mm
steps.description: In the Body Sizing Details → Definition, set Element Size to 3.175 mm.

verify.prompt: Body Sizing's Element Size shows 3.175 mm?

hints: Element Size is in the Definition section of the Body Sizing Details.

steps.title: Generate the final mesh
steps.description: Right-click Mesh in the Outline tree and choose Generate Mesh to apply the local Body Sizing on top of the global 6.35 mm size.
hints: The valve body should now appear visibly finer than the flanges/bolts in the Geometry view.

steps.title: Save the project
steps.description: File → Save Project, to save the meshed model for use later if desired.
hints: Click File to open the menu, then choose Save Project.
